claim: Explorer evidence verifies that boundary snapshots cover git tracked/cached/other files via git ls-files, include fallback walk for non-git projects, and exclude the artifact root from mutation detection, with test coverage confirming all three acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z, which covers tracked (default), cached (-c), and other/untracked (-o) files, returning null if git fails.
evidence.why: Verifies the primary claim that git-backed projects use git ls-files with the correct flags to capture all relevant file categories.

evidence.summary: walkFiles provides recursive filesystem walk fallback, excluding standard build/cache directories (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache).
evidence.why: Verifies the fallback walk mechanism for non-git projects and as supplement in git projects.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git and walk files with (gitFiles ? [...git, ...walk] : walkFiles), hashes each file with SHA-256, and excludes files within allowedRoots (artifact directories).
evidence.why: Verifies the core logic that combines both methods in git projects and uses walk only in non-git, while excluding allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test creates untracked-mutation.txt and expects blocked status with boundary violation and changes including the added file.
evidence.why: Verifies that git ls-files -o captures untracked files and the boundary detects them as mutations.

evidence.summary: Gitignored boundary test creates .env file and expects blocked status with boundary violation, confirming gitignored files outside excluded dirs are detected.
evidence.why: Verifies that gitignored files are still detected as project mutations, confirming git ls-files coverage.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test modifies src/existing.txt and creates agent-scratch.txt in runtimeDir; expects blocked status with src/existing.txt in changes but agent-scratch.txt excluded.
evidence.why: Verifies fallback walk detects modifications in non-git projects and artifact root is properly excluded from boundary checks.

evidence.summary: Execution policy declares allowed_write_roots as [repoRelative(outputDir, projectRoot)] and project_mutation as forbidden.
evidence.why: Verifies the documented contract that only the artifact output directory is allowed for writes during evidence gathering.

assumptions: git ls-files behavior is stable across common git versions for the -co flags used
Combination of git ls-files and walkFiles in git projects does not introduce significant overlap or gap edge cases
Test environment properly simulates real-world git state variations
risks: git ls-files behavior might differ across git versions or in edge cases like submodules/worktrees
Large non-git projects might hit performance limits with recursive walkFiles

result.notes: Explorer evidence is comprehensive and directly supports the acceptance criteria. Code review confirms git ls-files -co covers tracked/cached/other files, walk provides fallback for non-git, and allowedRoots excludes the artifact root. Test coverage validates all three scenarios: git untracked files, gitignored files, and non-git project modifications with artifact root exclusion. Assumptions are reasonable and properly documented; no critical evidence is missing.
